adodb_connection.pattern:
Dim conn As ADODB.Connection
Set conn = New ADODB.Connection
conn.ConnectionString = "Provider=Microsoft.Jet.OLEDB.4.0;Data Source=database.mdb"
conn.Open
' Use connection
conn.Close
Set conn = Nothing
adodb_connection.description: ADODB Connection converted to Entity Framework DbContext or SqlConnection

adodb_recordset_select.pattern:
Dim rs As ADODB.Recordset
Set rs = New ADODB.Recordset
rs.Open "SELECT * FROM Customers WHERE City = 'London'", conn, adOpenStatic
While Not rs.EOF
    Debug.Print rs("CustomerName")
    rs.MoveNext
Wend
rs.Close
Set rs = Nothing
adodb_recordset_select.description: ADODB Recordset SELECT query converted to LINQ

adodb_recordset_insert.pattern:
Dim rs As ADODB.Recordset
Set rs = New ADODB.Recordset
rs.Open "Customers", conn, adOpenDynamic, adLockOptimistic
rs.AddNew
rs("CustomerName") = "New Customer"
rs("City") = "New York"
rs.Update
rs.Close
adodb_recordset_insert.description: ADODB Recordset INSERT operation converted to Entity Framework Add

adodb_recordset_update.pattern:
Dim rs As ADODB.Recordset
Set rs = New ADODB.Recordset
rs.Open "SELECT * FROM Customers WHERE CustomerID = 1", conn, adOpenDynamic, adLockOptimistic
If Not rs.EOF Then
    rs("CustomerName") = "Updated Name"
    rs.Update
End If
rs.Close
adodb_recordset_update.description: ADODB Recordset UPDATE operation converted to Entity Framework

adodb_recordset_delete.pattern:
Dim rs As ADODB.Recordset
Set rs = New ADODB.Recordset
rs.Open "SELECT * FROM Customers WHERE CustomerID = 1", conn, adOpenDynamic, adLockOptimistic
If Not rs.EOF Then
    rs.Delete
End If
rs.Close
adodb_recordset_delete.description: ADODB Recordset DELETE operation converted to Entity Framework Remove

adodb_command.pattern:
Dim cmd As ADODB.Command
Set cmd = New ADODB.Command
cmd.ActiveConnection = conn
cmd.CommandText = "INSERT INTO Customers (CustomerName, City) VALUES (?, ?)"
cmd.Parameters.Append cmd.CreateParameter(, adVarChar, adParamInput, 50, "John Doe")
cmd.Parameters.Append cmd.CreateParameter(, adVarChar, adParamInput, 50, "Boston")
cmd.Execute
adodb_command.description: ADODB Command with parameters converted to Entity Framework

sql_string_concatenation.pattern: sql = "SELECT * FROM Customers WHERE CustomerName = '" & customerName & "' AND City = '" & city & "'"
sql_string_concatenation.description: SQL string concatenation converted to parameterized queries

data_type_conversion.pattern:
If Not IsNull(rs("BirthDate")) Then
    birthDate = CDate(rs("BirthDate"))
End If

If Not IsNull(rs("Salary")) Then
    salary = CDbl(rs("Salary"))
End If
data_type_conversion.description: Null handling and data type conversion

transaction_handling.pattern:
conn.BeginTrans
On Error GoTo RollbackTrans

' Multiple database operations
cmd1.Execute
cmd2.Execute
cmd3.Execute

conn.CommitTrans
Exit Sub

RollbackTrans:
conn.RollbackTrans
MsgBox "Transaction failed: " & Err.Description

stored_procedure_call.pattern:
Dim cmd As ADODB.Command
Set cmd = New ADODB.Command
cmd.ActiveConnection = conn
cmd.CommandText = "sp_GetCustomerOrders"
cmd.CommandType = adCmdStoredProc
cmd.Parameters.Append cmd.CreateParameter("@CustomerID", adInteger, adParamInput, , 123)
Set rs = cmd.Execute

database_schema_creation.pattern:
sql = "CREATE TABLE Customers (" & _
      "CustomerID COUNTER PRIMARY KEY, " & _
      "CustomerName TEXT(50) NOT NULL, " & _
      "City TEXT(50), " & _
      "Country TEXT(50))"
database_schema_creation.description: Database schema creation using Entity Framework attributes

recordset_navigation.pattern:
rs.MoveFirst
While Not rs.EOF
    Debug.Print rs("CustomerName")
    rs.MoveNext
Wend

rs.MoveLast
If rs.RecordCount > 0 Then
    lastRecord = rs("CustomerName")
End If
recordset_navigation.description: Recordset navigation converted to LINQ operations

field_properties.pattern:
For i = 0 To rs.Fields.Count - 1
    fieldName = rs.Fields(i).Name
    fieldType = rs.Fields(i).Type
    fieldSize = rs.Fields(i).DefinedSize
    Debug.Print fieldName & " - " & fieldType
Next i

data_binding.pattern:
Set DataGrid1.DataSource = rs
DataGrid1.Refresh

' Or with Data control
Data1.RecordSource = "SELECT * FROM Customers"
Data1.Refresh
Text1.DataSource = Data1
Text1.DataField = "CustomerName"

connection_pooling.pattern:
' VB6 doesn't have explicit connection pooling
' Connections were typically opened/closed for each operation
conn.Open
' Do work
conn.Close
connection_pooling.description: Connection pooling is automatic in Entity Framework

async_operations.pattern:
' VB6 operations were synchronous
rs.Open "SELECT * FROM LargeTable", conn
' This blocks until complete
async_operations.description: Converting synchronous operations to async/await pattern
